---
title: plot.visualization.plot_design_points
---



```python
plot.visualization.plot_design_points(
    X,
    i=0,
    j=1,
    agg='mean',
    show=True,
    **kwargs,
)
```

Plot design points projected onto two selected dimensions.

Displays a scatter plot of design points using dimensions ``i`` and ``j``
as the axes. When ``X`` has more than two dimensions, all remaining
dimensions (≠ ``i`` and ≠ ``j``) are aggregated per point using the
``agg`` function, and the result is used to colour the markers.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name     | Type                                     | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Default    |
|----------|------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| X        | [np](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) | Design matrix of shape ``(n_samples, n_dim)``. Each row is one design point.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | _required_ |
| i        | [int](`int`)                             | Index of the dimension to plot on the x-axis. Defaults to ``0``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | `0`        |
| j        | [int](`int`)                             | Index of the dimension to plot on the y-axis. Defaults to ``1``. Must differ from ``i`` when ``n_dim > 1``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | `1`        |
| agg      | [str](`str`) or [callable](`callable`)   | Aggregation applied to the dimensions that are *not* plotted (i.e. every column except ``i`` and ``j``). Supported strings: ``"mean"``, ``"median"``, ``"min"``, ``"max"``. A callable ``f(arr, axis)`` is also accepted (e.g. ``np.std``). The aggregated value is used as the colour of each marker so that spread across the hidden dimensions is visible at a glance. When ``n_dim == 2`` this parameter is ignored (no hidden dims). Defaults to ``"mean"``. | `'mean'`   |
| show     | [bool](`bool`)                           | If ``True``, call :func:`matplotlib.pyplot.show` before returning. Set to ``False`` when running inside tests or scripts that manage display manually. Defaults to ``True``.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | `True`     |
| **kwargs | [object](`object`)                       | Additional keyword arguments forwarded to :func:`matplotlib.pyplot.subplots` (e.g. ``figsize``) and to :func:`matplotlib.axes.Axes.scatter` (e.g. ``s``, ``cmap``, ``alpha``).  ``figsize`` is intercepted and passed to ``subplots``; all other keys go to ``scatter``.                                                                                                                                                                                          | `{}`       |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type               | Description                                                      |
|--------|--------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [object](`object`) | matplotlib.figure.Figure: The created figure object. Call        |
|        | [object](`object`) | ``plt.show()`` or ``fig.savefig(...)`` on the returned object as |
|        | [object](`object`) | needed.                                                          |

## Raises {.doc-section .doc-section-raises}

| Name   | Type                         | Description                                                                                             |
|--------|------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [ImportError](`ImportError`) | If *matplotlib* is not installed.                                                                       |
|        | [ValueError](`ValueError`)   | If ``X`` is not a 2-D array, has fewer than two columns, or if ``i`` / ``j`` are out of range or equal. |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}


::: {#c8242346 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.function import sphere
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_design_points

opt = SpotOptim(
    fun=sphere,
    bounds=[(-5, 5), (-5, 5)],
    n_initial=10,
    seed=42,
)
# Generate a Latin-hypercube initial design (in original scale)
X0 = opt.get_initial_design()

# 2-D design – simple scatter on both dimensions
fig = plot_design_points(X0, i=0, j=1, figsize=(5, 4))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization.plot_design_points_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=469 height=373}
:::
:::


::: {#75d977c8 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np
from spotoptim import SpotOptim
from spotoptim.function import sphere
from spotoptim.plot.visualization import plot_design_points

opt = SpotOptim(
    fun=sphere,
    bounds=[(-5, 5)] * 4,
    n_initial=20,
    seed=42,
)
X0 = opt.get_initial_design()

# 4-D design – colour encodes the mean of the two hidden dimensions
fig = plot_design_points(X0, i=0, j=1, agg="mean", figsize=(6, 5), s=60)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](plot.visualization.plot_design_points_files/figure-html/cell-3-output-1.png){width=557 height=469}
:::
:::
